summary: WhatTheFuel separates presentation from evidence. Prices, news, and timeline entries are traced to official pages and selected newsroom sources.
automation: The frontend reads JSON from /public/data so the pages can render without a runtime server.
Live news refreshes from approved feeds before deployment and on a weekly schedule.
If a source fetch fails, the checked-in JSON stays in place instead of fabricating live data.
editorialRules: Only publish an item if it links to a real source URL.
Keep explainers, news, and policy snapshots clearly separated.
Prefer a blank state over a confident but unverifiable claim.
Keep live feed items in the source language when that is how the source publishes them.
limitations: Live news is limited to approved sources and fuel-related keyword matches.
Long-form pages are translated, but live feed items stay in the original source language.
Some fuel-price figures update on different MOF pages, so each snapshot keeps its own source and date.

approvedSources.name: Ministry of Finance Malaysia
approvedSources.purpose: Fuel pricing, subsidy policy, quota rules, and public statements

approvedSources.name: Department of Statistics Malaysia
approvedSources.purpose: Malaysia macro, energy trade, and sector data used in explainers

approvedSources.name: Free Malaysia Today
approvedSources.purpose: Malaysia national, business, and world reporting used for live fuel-related coverage

approvedSources.name: Malay Mail
approvedSources.purpose: Malaysia and world reporting used for live fuel-related coverage

approvedSources.name: Berita RTM
approvedSources.purpose: Malay-language national coverage used for live fuel-related reporting

approvedSources.name: Sin Chew Daily
approvedSources.purpose: Chinese-language Malaysia and world reporting used for live fuel-related coverage

approvedSources.name: Sinar Premium
approvedSources.purpose: Malay-language coverage used for live fuel-related reporting

approvedSources.name: BBC News and other reviewed outlets
approvedSources.purpose: Live fuel, oil, shipping, and energy reporting when a newsroom source is needed

approvedSources.name: U.S. Energy Information Administration
approvedSources.purpose: Global oil-market structure, chokepoints, prices, and market mechanics

approvedSources.name: Reuters and other reviewed outlets
approvedSources.purpose: Attributable fuel and energy reporting used when the story needs editorial context or a linked quotation
